DECLARATION OF FREDERICK J. SETTE IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 210077137) FILED BY PLAINTIFF DALTON, DEBORAH , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM FOR GREGORY DALTON
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-20-2024 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-20-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DEBORAH DALTON'S MOTION TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. "PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT" IS GRANTED. "THAT TRIAL COURTS ARE TO LIBERALLY PERMIT SUCH AMENDMENTS, AT ANY STAGE OF THE PROCEEDING, HAS BEEN ESTABLISHED POLICY IN THIS STATE SINCE 1901." (HIRSA V. SUP. CT. (1981) 118 CAL.APP.3D 486, 488-89.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER STATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Motion And Notice Of Motion To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DEBORAH DALTON's Motion To File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

"Plaintiffs' motion to file first amended complaint" is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
